title: Extraction of alarm substance in zebrafish
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cnc6g","text":"Alarm substance, or Schrekcstoff, is an mixture of compounds produced by specialized club cells in the epidermis of Ostariophysan fish. When lesioned, these cells release the substance, which acts as an aversive olfactory stimulus capable of evoking intense behavioral responses (alarm reaction) in other fish in the shoal (Maximino et al. 2018). Alarm substance is used as an aversive stimulus to study fear-like behavior in Ostariophysan - especially in zebrafish. The present protocol describes a technique to easily extract alarm substance for acute exposure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9kcsf","text":"Guidelines for humane euthanasia must be followed, and animals should NEVER be euthanized without deep anesthesia. See Matthews \u0026 Varga (doi: 10.1093/ilar.53.2.192) for a review.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":142,"length":21}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"685EEFEFBC4E49F7B1228645C1CB4397","url":"https://doi.org/10.1093/ilar.53.2.192"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"5n5q8","text":"1. Alarm substance extraction begins by cutting the fish skin with a scalpel; therefore, care must be taken to avoid accidents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdj28","text":"2. All manipulations must be made using surgical gloves, to reduce the risk of contamination by aquatic bacteria (e.g., Mycobacterium marinum)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Separate a donor fish from the home tank and transport to the experiment area
Make crushed ice using cubes made from mineral water Ice Contributed by users
Fill a 2L beaker with water from the fish facility Graduated Plastic Beakers, 2000ml Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #BK044-2K.SIZE.1
Bring the water temperature to 17 °C by adding the ice chips
Use a net to transfer the fish from the container to the cold water
Slowly add ice chips to the beaker to bring the temperature down to 12 °C . The animal is considered anesthetized when it no longer reacts to gentle manipulation.
Transfer the anesthetized fish to a Petri dish Glass Petri dishes 90 x 15 cm Contributed by users
Quickly decapitate the animal using a scapel; discard the head
Remove excess blood using a swab Cotton Gauze Swabs (10 x 10cm x 8 ply) Contributed by users Catalog #G5201 Emh40/0404
Transfer the body to another Petri dish
Using a scalpel, make 15 superficial cuts in both sides of the trunk scalpel blades Contributed by users
Wash the body with 10 ml ddH2O Distilled Water Contributed by users
Remove scales and other impurities from the solution
Transfer 7 ml from the solution to a Falcon tube 15 ml sterile falcon tubes and rack Contributed by users
Store on ice or refrigerator for up to 4 h.
creator: Caio Maximino
